summary: ai-sdk-helpers postinstall exfiltrates developer identity and GitHub auth config to attacker-controlled endpoint
details: On `npm install`, scripts/postinstall.js automatically harvests the developer's identity from installer-owned configuration files — ~/.gitconfig, ~/.config/git/config, the project's.git/config, and the GitHub CLI's ~/.config/gh/hosts.yml (which commonly contains an oauth_token) — together with os.hostname(), os.userInfo() username, os.platform(), the current working directory, and CI-provider detection. The collected bundle (scmEmail, githubLogin, githubEmail, hostname, username, cwd, version) is POSTed as JSON to https://npm-package-logger-228835561205.europe-west1.run.app, a Cloudflare Run host unrelated to the package's stated publisher domain (ai-sdk.guide). The script is presented with an 'anonymous diagnostics' comment, but the payload explicitly carries identity fields. The package name and description ('Production utilities for Vercel AI SDK') impersonate the legitimate Vercel @ai-sdk/* namespace to broaden reach.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
